titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/196

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=449 status=live modified=2021-04-06 15:46:24.88747+00 | translation moa=450 status=live modified=2021-04-06 15:46:23.175879+00

div.p:
Omnibus sancte Matris ecclesie filiis Geruasius Auenel filius Roberti Auenel Salutem . Sciatis me concedente et consentiente Geruasio filio meo et herede concessisse et hac Mea carta confir
masse deo et ecclesie sancte Marie de Melros et Monachis ibidem deo seruientibus donacionem patris mei . scilicet terram terram de eskedale quam idem pater meus illam donauit in perpetuam elemosinam
annuente piissimo Rege Malcolmo prius . et postea illustri Rege willelmo fratre eius . scilicet Tumlouher et Watkerroc in liberam . puram . et perpetuam elemosinam . pro anima gloriosi Regis dauid
qui eandem terram donauerat eidem patri meo pro seruitio suo . et pro anima comitis Henrici et predicti Malcolmi Regis . et pro anima predicti willelmi Regis et alexandri filii eius Regis scottorum
domini mei . et omnium antecessorum et successorum eius . et pro anima mea et uxoris mee et omnium successorum meorum . Hanc itaque scilicet Tumlouher et Watkerroch concessi pre
dictis Monachis . et dedi . et confirmaui . et hac carta me incartaui per has diuisas . videlicet sicut duo eskes conueniunt . et ita sursum per blac eske usque ad primum
torrentem qui descendit de hertesfel . et de illo torrente sursum per medium condos montium inter ipsos et Robert de brus . sicut aque descendit uersus ipsos Monachos . et ita inter fore
stam de tymey et ipsos per medium condos montium sicut aque descendit uersus ipsos . Similiter inter terram de bordwich et ipsos Monachos per medium condos montium sicut aque descendit
uersus ipsos . et ita usque ad terram de Hawic . et inde transuersum per medium condos montium quod esse inter meipsum et predictos Monachos sicut aque decendit uersus ipsos . Quod condos inclu
dit Harewde uersus Monachos et ita se extendit ex orientali parte de ipso Harewde et sic semper descendendo usque ubi duo eskes conueniunt . Totam autem hanc prenomi
natam terram et possessionem . et quicquid continetur infra has predictas diuisas in bosco et plano . in pratis et pasturis . in aquis et mariscis . et in omnibus utilitatibus et aisiamentis
concessi et dedi confirmaui antedictis Monachis in puram et perpetuam elemosinam perpetuo iure tenendam et possidendam liberam et quietam a me et heredibus meis et solutam ab
omnibus seruiciis . et ab omni consuetudine et exaccione seculari . sicut liberius et quietius . plenius et honorificentius aliqua elemosina teneri et possideri potest . Et siquis forte aliquando hanc predictam
terram calumniari uoluerit  ego et heredes mei omni calumnianti respondebimus . et sufficienter defendemus eam predictis Monachis et warantizabimus . Hoc autem sciendum est quod mihi et heredibus meis retineo tantum . ceruum et ceruam . porcum et caperolum infra prenominatas diuisas . absque damno tamen et lesione segetum . pratorum . sepium . pecudum . animalium . et omnium aliarum
possessionum eorundem Monachorum . Ita tamen quod ego et heredes mei forestarium non habebimus infra predictas diuisas . verumptamen habebimus infra easdem diuisas latrones . et accipitrum et speruiroum
nidos et custodes ad illos custodiendos . et forsueias . Et sciendum quod Monachi predicti habebunt et possidebunt totam predictam terram liberam et quietam a me et heredibus meis salua predicta salua
gina mea et nidis accipitrum et sperueriorum . Et ipsi monachi utentur omni hoc tenemento . et facient logias ubicumque uoluerint pro libitu et uoluntate sua sicut melius et utilius sibi et fratribus suis
seruientibus et aueriis suis proficere uisum fuerit . Excepto quod non uenabuntur ibi cum motis et cordis . nec alios ibi ducent ad uenandum . nec pedicas ibi ponent nisi ad capiendos lupos . Neque
accipient infra has diuisas accipitrum et speruiorum nidos . Preterea sciendum quod quinque Marchas quas aliquando pater meus in firmam accepit ab eis quasque ipse et mater illis ante mortem
suam quietas clamauerunt  has ego similiter quietas clamaui  et eis in elemosinam dedi . et inperpetuum remisi . et hoc idem presenti carta incartaui . Et ut hec elemosina stabilis
et firma sit in perpetuum . nec ullo modo possit in irritum duci  Dominus meus willelmus Rex scottorum peticione et rogatu meo eandem elemosinam eis concessit et dedit . et carta sua confir
mauit . Testibus . Henr’ . abbate . de kelchou . Sibilla uxore mea . Geruasio filio meo et herede . Waltero olifard . Willelmo filio iohannis . Thoma de sumeruilla . Iohanne de maccuswel . Ric
ardo le neim . Bernardo de haud’ . Rogero burnard . Gregorio de rutherford . Ricardo clerico meo . Roberto filio meo . Adam filio Rogeri . et aliis mvltis .

To all sons of holy Mother Church, Gervase Avenel, son of Robert Avenel, sends greeting. Know me, Gervase my son and heir agreeing and consenting, to have granted and by this my charter to have made firm to God and the church of Saint Mary at Melrose, and to the Monks serving God in that place, the donation my father; that is, the land of Eskdale which my same father donated to them in everlasting alms, the most pious King Malcolm first approving, and afterwards the illustrious King William, his brother; to wit, Tomleuchar and Watcarrick, in free, pure, and everlasting alms; for the soul of the glorious King David, who had donated the same land to my same father in return for his service; and for the soul of Earl Henry and of the aforesaid King Malcolm, and for the soul of the said King William and of Alexander, the son of the king of Scots, my lord, and of all his ancestors' and successors' [souls], and for my own soul and of my wife's [soul], and of all my ancestors' and successors' [souls]. I have therefore granted this, to wit, Tomleuchar and Watcarrik, to the aforesaid monks, and have given and confirmed and described in a written deed by this my charter, by these boundaries, namely, as the two Esks meet, so upwards by the Black Esk as far as the first stream that descends from Hartside; and from that stream, upwards through the middle ridge of the mountains, between them and Robert de Brus, as the waters descend towards the monks themselves; and so between the forest of Tymey and themselves, by the middle ridge of the mountains, as they descend towards themselves; similarly between the land of Borthwick and the monks themselves, by the middle ridge of the mountains, as the waters descend towards them; and so as far as the land of Hawick, and thence across by the middle ridge of the mountains which is between myself and the aforesaid monks, as the waters descend towards them; this ridge encloses Harewde towards the monks, and so descends from the east side from Harewde itself, and so always descending to where the two Esks meet. And this whole aforenamed land, possession, and whatever is contained within these aforesaid boundaries, in wood and plain, in meadows and pastures, in waters and marshes, and in all benefits and easements, I have granted and given, and made firm to the aforesaid monks, in pure and everlasting alms, to be held and possessed by perpetual right, free and quit from me and my heirs, and released from all services, and from every custom and secular exaction, as freely and quitely, fully and honourably, as any alms is able to be held and possessed. And if by chance anyone should wish to claim the aforesaid alms, I and my heirs shall answer all claimants, and sufficiently protect and warrant it for the aforesaid monks. This is also to be known: that I have retained for myself and my heirs only deer and hind, swine and roebuck, within the aforenamed boundaries, without harm and injury of crops, meadows, hedges, livestock, animals, and all the other possessions of the same monks; provided, however, that I and my heirs shall not have a forester within the described boundaries, nevertheless we shall have within the same boundaries robbers, and eyries of hawks and sparrow-hawks, and the guards to guard them, and trespassers; and it should be known that the aforesaid monks shall have and possess the whole aforesaid land, free and quit from me and my heirs, saving my aforesaid wild game, and the eyries of hawks and sparrow-hawks; and the monks shall make use of all this tenement according to their will and pleasure, just as it shall seem to them best and most useful to be of use to themselves, their brethren, their servants and livestock. With the exception that they shall not hunt there with packs of hounds and ropes, nor bring others there for hunting, nor set traps there, unless for catching wolves; and they shall not take the eyries of hawks and sparrow-hawks within these boundaries. It should be known in addition that the five merks which at one time my father received in ferme from them, and which he and my mother quitclaimed to them before their death; this I likewise have quitclaimed, and given to them in alms, and have remitted for ever, and this same [alms] I have [confirmed] by the present charter. And that this alms should be established and firm for ever, nor could by any means be declared invalid, the lord William, king of Scots, by my petition and request has granted and given the same alms to them, and confirmed by his charter. As witnesses, Henry, abbot of Kelso, Sibyl, my wife, Gervase my son and heir, Walter Oliphant, William son of John, Thomas de Somerville, John of Maxwell, Richard le Neym, Bernard of Howden, Roger Burnard, Gregory of Rutherford, Richard, my clerk, Robert my son, Adam son of Roger, and many others.
